title: tomophyllum capillatum
intro: Tomophyllum capillatum, also known as the 'Hair-like fern', is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The plant is believed to provide various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating respiratory issues, making it a valuable resource in traditional herbal remedies. Tomophyllum capillatum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, allowing for its medicinal properties to be harnessed and utilized for various health purposes.
